task: cross-INSTANCE role transfer on a2a_mcp (Phase 2 — deployable-attack test)
hypothesis: two independent instances of the SAME framework share call structure and differ only in surface variables (LLM, prompts, session, driver), so role transfer SHOULD work — a prediction, not a guarantee.

independence_of_instance2.different_driver_for_specialists: instance-2's specialist samples were topped up with the fan-out-BOOSTED driver (drive_orch_boost.py) + fully-specified queries, because a2a_mcp's natural fan-out to air/hotel/car is only ~6-11% of trips. This is an ADDED axis of difference (cuts both ways — see driver_confound_interpretation and specialist_distribution_check); the coordinator samples remain natural.
independence_of_instance2.shared: the a2a_mcp framework's fixed six roles by port (10100-10105)

representation: 35-dim per-agent traffic-shape vector (features/per_flow.py); port is the LABEL only, never a feature.
method: fit GBT on train instance, predict test instance (_transfer pattern); macro-F1 with bootstrap 95% CI (seed 42); chance = 1/n_test_roles.

coordinator_layer_3way_clean.note: NATURAL both instances (coordinators fire on every trip; no boosted driver) — the clean, unconfounded deployable result.
coordinator_layer_3way_clean.verdict: DEPLOYABLE ATTACK (≥0.70, CI clear of chance)

coordinator_vs_specialist.caveat: PARTLY STRUCTURAL — hubs carry more traffic than leaves, so this rides on connection volume (like topology), not subtle per-agent behaviour. The behavioural headline is the multi-role transfer above.

driver_confound_interpretation: 6-way <0.70 with specialists in the transfer. The boosted driver used for inst-2 specialists is a POSSIBLE CONTRIBUTOR to the drop (train-natural / test-forced mismatch), NOT necessarily 'behaviour doesn't transfer'. This must be named as a candidate confound. Specialist distributions differ across instances, consistent with the driver contributing — the structure-vs-behaviour reading is confounded here.
verdict_phase2: PARTIAL (0.40–0.70; transfers but instance drift degrades it)
verdict_basis: weaker of the two directions (brief §4); verdict field matches the number.
caveats: Single second instance. Specialist samples (air/hotel/car) in instance-2 were collected with the boosted driver (see independence_of_instance2 + specialist_distribution_check); coordinator samples are natural.
